chapter_title.ar: باب كَرَاهِيَةِ أَنْ تَتَبَتَّلَ النِّسَاءُ وَ يُعَطِّلْنَ أَنْفُسَهُنَّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 147 – Abhorrence of the women being celibate and delaying themselves (for marriage)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/322)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/322)
text: لَا يَنْبَغِي لِلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ تُعَطِّلَ نَفْسَهَا وَ لَوْ تُعَلِّقُ فِي عُنُقِهَا قِلَادَةً وَ لَا يَنْبَغِي أَنْ تَدَعَ يَدَهَا مِنَ الْخِضَابِ وَ لَوْ تَمْسَحُهَا مَسْحاً بِالْحِنَّاءِ وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ مُسِنَّةً .

en.hubeali: Ibn Mahboub, from Al A’ala, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It is not befitting for the woman that she neglects herself, even if she attaches a necklace in her neck, nor is it befitting that she leaves her hand from the dyeing even if she wipes it with a touch with henna, even if she was old’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ibn Mahbub has narrated from al-‘ Ala’ from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is not proper for women to neglect themselves even from hanging a necklace from their necks. It is not proper for women to leave their hands without dye even if they are old.’”
